CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

ACRONYM TABLE 
CMCC Centro Euro-Mediterraneo sui Cambiamenti Climatici 
CMEMS Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service 
CTD Conductivity, Temperature, and Depth; It refers to a package of electronic instru-
ments that measure these properties 
EAN Estimated Accuracy Number 
ECMWF European Centre for Medium range Weather Forecasting 
EOF Estimated Accuracy Number 
EUR Europe 
GEBCO General Bathymetric Chart of the Oceans  
GLO Global 
GODAE Global Ocean Data Assimilation Experiment 
Med  Mediterranean 
MFC Monitoring and Forecasting Center 
MLD Mixed Layer Depth 
MY Multi-Year 
NEMO Nucleus for European Modelling of the Ocean  
NRT Near Real Time 
PHY PHYsics  
PQ Product Quality 
RMSD Root Mean Square Difference 
S Salinity 
SL Sea Level 
SLA Sea Level Anomaly 
 
QUID for MED MFC Product 
MEDSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_006_004 
Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
CMEMS-MED-QUID-006-004 
28 May 2025 
2.4 
 
        Page 4/ 67 
 
SST Sea Surface Temperature 
T Temperature 
TAC Thematic Assembly Center 
UV Horizontal (U=zonal, V=Meridional) currents 
XBT Expendable Bathythermograph 
 
KEY DEFINITIONS 
Argo International profiling float network (www.argo.net)  
Climatology Long-term average of a variable over a reference period. 
Eddy diffusivity and 
viscosity coefficients 
Physical parameters representing energy dissipation at horizontal and 
vertical turbulence scales. 
Eddy kinetic energy Kinetic energy associated with the turbulent components of the ve-
locity field. 
Hovmöller diagram Diagram showing how a field changes over time along a spatial dimen-
sion, such as depth. 
Mixed layer depth Depth of the upper ocean layer homogeneously mixed by wind and 
surface fluxes, defined as the shallowest depth where potential den-
sity increases by 1% relative to its value at 10 m depth. 
Sverdrup (Sv) A unit commonly used to express ocean volume transport  
(1 Sv = 10⁶ m³ s⁻¹).
